tools.name: vidiq_keyword_research
tools.title: Keyword Research
tools.description:
Research YouTube keywords to find search volume, competition, and related keyword opportunities. This tool returns keyword metrics, NOT videos. To find actual videos, use vidiq_outliers or vidiq_trending_videos instead.

This tool has three modes — choose with the `mode` parameter based on what the user is asking for:

1. `research` (default): full keyword metrics for a seed keyword plus related keyword suggestions. Use for global/topic research ("research minecraft tips", "keyword ideas for cooking"). Returns for each keyword:
   - Volume (0-100): How often people search for this keyword on YouTube
   - Competition (0-100): How many videos target this keyword
   - Overall score (0-100): Combined keyword opportunity score
   - Estimated monthly search volume
   - Top markets: the countries where the keyword is most searched, with each country's share of searches
   - In-country volume: optionally pass a `country` (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2) to also get each keyword's estimated search volume within that country (the `countryVolume` field)

2. `country_search`: the top keywords matching a TOPIC within ONE country, ranked by in-country search volume and capped at `limit`. Use when the user names BOTH a topic AND a country — e.g. "give me the top 10 minecraft keywords in IN" → mode='country_search', keyword='minecraft', country='IN', limit=10. Requires `country` and `keyword`. Results come back in `relatedKeywords` (each row's `countryVolume` is the in-country volume; global YouTube metrics are null).
   - By default (`broad: false`) this is an EXACT/PHRASE match — every returned keyword literally contains the topic words (e.g. 'minecraft' → 'minecraft mods', 'minecraft house').
   - Set `broad: true` for a SEMANTIC match: the topic is first expanded into related keywords (the engine behind vidIQ's "Related Keywords" tab), then those are ranked by in-country volume. Returned keywords need NOT contain the topic words (e.g. 'minecraft' → 'redstone', 'survival mode'). Use this when the user wants a bigger/wider list, says "related"/"similar"/"broaden", or the exact-match list is too small.

3. `country_top`: the top keywords overall within ONE country (NO topic filter), ranked by in-country search volume and capped at `limit`. Use when the user names a country but no topic — e.g. "what are the top keywords in Brazil" → mode='country_top', country='BR'. Requires `country`; `keyword` is ignored. Results come back in `relatedKeywords` as above.

Cost: 5 credit(s) per call.

mode.description:
Which kind of keyword research to run. Pick based on the user's question:
- 'research' (default): full metrics for a seed keyword plus related keyword suggestions (volume, competition, overall score, top markets). Use for global/topic research like 'research minecraft tips' or 'keyword ideas for cooking'. Optionally pass `country` to also enrich every row with its in-country search volume.
- 'country_search': the top keywords matching a topic WITHIN one country, ranked by in-country search volume. Use when the user names BOTH a topic AND a country, e.g. 'give me the top 10 minecraft keywords in IN' (keyword='minecraft', country='IN', limit=10). Requires `country` and `keyword`. By default this is an exact/phrase match; set `broad: true` for a semantic match that also returns related keywords not containing the topic words (a bigger list).
- 'country_top': the top keywords overall within one country, ranked by in-country search volume, with NO topic filter. Use when the user names a country but no topic, e.g. 'what are the top keywords in Brazil'. Requires `country`; `keyword` is ignored.

keyword.description: Seed keyword / topic (for example 'minecraft tips'). Required for 'research' and 'country_search' modes; ignored for 'country_top'.

includeRelated.description: Include related keyword suggestions ('research' mode only)

country.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code (for example 'US', 'IN'). In 'research' mode this is optional and enriches each keyword with its estimated in-country search volume (the `countryVolume` field). REQUIRED for 'country_top' and 'country_search' modes.

limit.description: Maximum number of keywords to return for 'country_top' and 'country_search' modes (the ranked list is capped at this size). Ignored in 'research' mode.

broad.description:
Only applies to 'country_search' mode. Controls how the topic is matched within the country:
- false (default) = EXACT/PHRASE match: returns only keywords whose text actually contains the topic words. E.g. topic 'minecraft' returns 'minecraft', 'minecraft mods', 'minecraft house' — every result literally contains 'minecraft'. Use when the user wants variants of a specific phrase.
- true = SEMANTIC/BROAD match: first expands the topic into semantically-related keywords (the same engine behind vidIQ's 'Related Keywords' tab), THEN ranks them by in-country search volume. Results need NOT contain the topic words — e.g. topic 'minecraft' can return 'redstone', 'survival mode', 'building ideas'. Use when the user wants a BIGGER / wider list of related ideas, says 'related', 'similar', 'broaden', 'more than just X', or complains the exact-match list is too small.

mode.description: Which research mode produced these results.

country.description: The country (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2) results are scoped/enriched for, or null if none.

query.description: The topic/phrase searched for in 'country_search' mode, or null otherwise.

countryVolume.description: Estimated search volume for this keyword within the requested country. Null when no country was requested, or when the keyword has no data in that country.

pct.description: Share of searches from this country, as a fraction in [0, 1]

topMarkets.description: Top countries by search share, ranked descending. Empty when no country-distribution data is available for this keyword.

seedKeyword.description: Full metrics for the seed keyword in 'research' mode. Null in country list modes ('country_top' / 'country_search').

relatedKeywords.description: In 'research' mode: related keyword suggestions. In 'country_top'/'country_search' modes: the ranked list of in-country keywords (only `keyword` and `countryVolume` are populated; the YouTube global metrics are null because the country index stores in-country volume only).

tools.name: vidiq_outliers
tools.title: Video Outliers
tools.description:
Find viral, breakout, and overperforming YouTube videos — videos getting significantly more views than their channel's average. Use this when someone asks for: viral videos, breakout hits, hidden gems, or videos blowing up, or when they want outliers from a specific creator or set of competitor channels (pass their channel IDs via `channelIds`). Returns a breakout score showing how much each video exceeds its channel's typical performance.

Cost: 5 credit(s) per call.

keyword.description: Search keyword to find outlier videos by title. Optional when `channelIds` is provided.

channelIds.description: Restrict results to one or more YouTube channels. Each entry may be a channel ID (UC...), a handle (@MrBeast or MrBeast), or a channel URL — handles and URLs are resolved to channel IDs automatically. Useful for finding outliers from a specific creator or a set of competitor channels. May be combined with `keyword`. At least one of `keyword` or `channelIds` must be supplied.

trendCategories.description: Filter by trend category IDs (e.g. reaction, tier_list, mukbang). Use vidiq_trend_categories to list all available categories.

tools.name: vidiq_channel_stats
tools.title: Channel Stats
tools.description:
Get YouTube channel statistics including subscriber count, total views, video count, and growth over a configurable time period.

Use this tool to:
- Look up any YouTube channel's current stats (subscribers, views, videos)
- See how much a channel has grown over a given period
- Get channel metadata (country, language, topics, creation date)

Cost: 5 credit(s) per call.

from.description: Start date (YYYY-MM-DD). Defaults to 30 days ago

tools.name: vidiq_trending_videos
tools.title: Trending Videos
tools.description:
Find YouTube videos that are trending right now — gaining views rapidly based on views-per-hour velocity. Use this when someone asks: what's trending, what's hot right now, popular videos this week, or fastest-growing videos. Unlike vidiq_outliers (which measures performance vs channel average), trending measures absolute velocity — high VPH regardless of channel size.

Use this tool to discover:
- Videos that are trending right now on YouTube
- Trending content in specific niches via title search
- High-performing videos filtered by views per hour, engagement, or subscriber range

Cost: 5 credit(s) per call.

videoFormat.description: Video format: 'long' for regular videos, 'short' for YouTube Shorts

titleQuery.description: Search query to filter by video title (semantic search)

channelCountry.description: Filter by channel country (2-letter code, e.g. 'US')

videoPublishedAfter.description: Only videos published after this date (YYYY-MM-DD)

videoPublishedBefore.description: Only videos published before this date (YYYY-MM-DD)

tools.name: vidiq_breakout_channels
tools.title: Breakout Channels
tools.description:
DEPRECATED — prefer vidiq_channel_search with breakoutChannel: true, which returns the same emerging-channel ranking plus richer filters (language, growth windows, per-format metrics) and a fuller output. This tool remains for backward compatibility only.

Discover YouTube channels by niche or topic. Find breakout channels with growth metrics and audience data. Returns channels, not individual videos. To find viral videos across channels, use vidiq_outliers.

Use this tool to:
- Search for channels in a specific niche (e.g. 'cooking tutorials', 'tech reviews')
- Filter by content type (long-form, shorts, or mixed)
- Filter by country, subscriber count range, and faceless channel status
- See channel growth metrics (30-day subscriber growth)

Cost: 5 credit(s) per call.

query.description: Search query to find YouTube channels by niche (e.g. 'cooking tutorials', 'tech reviews')

country.description: Filter by channel country (2-letter code, e.g. 'US')

faceless.description: Filter by faceless channel status. Use true for faceless channels, false for non-faceless channels.

tools.name: vidiq_channel_search
tools.title: Channel Search
tools.description:
Comprehensive YouTube channel search. Combines semantic + lexical text search with structured filters across identity, audience size, growth, format mix, and content metadata.

Use this tool to:
- Find channels by niche or topic with natural-language queries (semantic match on niche + description embeddings).
- Look up a specific channel by YouTube handle (e.g. '@MrBeast').
- Require specific wording in the channel description with the description filter (consecutive words, typo-tolerant) — for 'channels whose description mentions/says X'.
- Filter by channel size (subscriber/view/video count ranges), growth (7d / 30d / 1y subs and views), recency (last video published), format (long / short / mixed), per-format average duration and video/view counts (7d / 30d / 1y), creation date, country, language, main category, and flags (faceless, breakout).
- Order results with the optional sort param (relevance / subscriberCount / subsGrowth30d / viewsGrowth30d / lastVideoPublished). Topical asks — including 'top/popular/biggest channels about X' — use relevance (default), which already favors notable channels; field sorts are ONLY for explicit metric scans and re-order by the raw metric, ignoring topical fit.
- Combine any subset of inputs — at least one of query / handle / channel title / filters is required.
- Find emerging channels: set breakoutChannel: true for any 'breakout / emerging / up-and-coming channels in X' intent — the flag filters to breakout channels AND switches ranking to favor small fast-rising channels (replaces vidiq_breakout_channels).
- Non-English / local discovery: pass the language or country as a FILTER, never translate the query — 'дай каналы про уроки макияжа' → query: 'уроки макияжа', languages: ['ru']; 'tokyo street food' → query: 'tokyo street food', country: ['JP']; 'news and politics in Ukraine' → country: ['UA'] (news/politics/local-events about a country means that country's channels, not foreign coverage). Results should match the request's language unless the user says otherwise. For geo-local or language-specific discovery request limit 20+ — relevant local channels may rank below globally popular ones.

For finding COMPETITORS of a channel — the user's own ("who are my competitors?") or a named one ("channels similar to Creator Insider") — prefer vidiq_similar_channels (auto-exclusion and competitor-tuned scoring); this tool is still the right first step there, to resolve the reference channel's niche, subNiches, subscriberCount, country, and languages. For viral-video discovery, use vidiq_outliers.

Cost: 5 credit(s) per call.

query.description: Free-text query describing the niche, topic, or content (e.g. 'vegan cooking tutorials', 'tech reviews', 'minecraft let's plays'). Drives semantic search across niches and description embeddings plus lexical match on niches/description. Write the query in the LANGUAGE THE USER USED — NEVER translate their wording to English. The search is multilingual; translating discards the language signal and returns the wrong locale's channels. Non-English request → keep the original wording here AND set `languages` to the request's language. Optional — you can also run a pure metrics/attribute search by providing only filters.

channelTitle.description: Optional channel title fragment to bias results toward channels whose title matches (e.g. 'Mark Rober').

channelTitleMatch.description: How to match channelTitle. 'fuzzy' (default) ranks by title similarity (partial matches allowed). 'exact' restricts to channels whose title equals the value (case-insensitive). Requires channelTitle — rejected if set without it.

handle.description: YouTube handle / custom URL (e.g. '@MrBeast' or 'mrbeast'). Leading '@' is optional. Use this when you want a specific channel.

handleMatch.description: How to match handle. 'exact' (default) returns only the channel with that exact handle. 'fuzzy' does a partial/relevance match (e.g. 'beast' matches '@MrBeast'). Requires handle — rejected if set without it.

description.description: Require a phrase in the channel description: the words must appear consecutively, in order, each tolerating small typos. A hard filter (restricts the result set, works with any sort), unlike `query` which only ranks. Use when the user wants channels whose DESCRIPTION says something specific (e.g. description: 'redstone tutorials' or 'business inquiries'). For topic/niche discovery, prefer `query`.

faceless.description: Filter by faceless-channel status. true returns only faceless channels, false excludes them.

breakoutChannel.description: Filter by breakout-channel flag. true also switches ranking to emerging-channel mode: the popularity prior is disabled so small fast-rising channels rank by pure relevance instead of being outranked by established ones. Use breakoutChannel: true for any 'breakout / emerging / up-and-coming channels in X' intent. Omit to return both breakout and non-breakout channels with default ranking.

country.description: Filter by channel country (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 codes, uppercase, e.g. ['US', 'GB']). Multiple codes are OR-ed. When the query names a place ('tokyo street food', 'nyc daily vlogs'), pass the corresponding country code(s) — e.g. tokyo → ['JP'] — and keep the city name in the query. For news, politics, or local-events queries about a country ('news and politics in Ukraine'), ALWAYS set that country (usually with its language in `languages`): the audience wants that country's channels, not foreign channels covering it. Skip the country filter only for travel/culture coverage intent where creators from anywhere qualify ('travel vlogs about Japan').

languages.description: Filter by channel language(s) (ISO 639-1 codes, lowercase, e.g. ['en', 'es']). When the USER'S REQUEST is written in a non-English language, or they ask for content in a specific language ('на русском', 'auf deutsch'), ALWAYS pass that language here — e.g. query 'уроки макияжа' → languages: ['ru']. Results should match the request's language unless the user says otherwise. Do not translate the query; the search understands non-English text, but without this filter big channels in other languages will outrank local ones. By default matches any channel that *contains* a requested language (multilingual channels included). Set exactLanguage=true to return only monolingual channels whose language is exactly the requested value.

exactLanguage.description: When true, match languages exactly (only monolingual channels whose single language equals the requested code). Prefer true when the user wants content purely in that language — multilingual channels (e.g. 'hi, en') often produce content mostly in their other language. When false/omitted, match any channel containing the requested language(s). Requires a non-empty `languages` list — rejected if true without it.

mainCategory.description: Filter by main category (YouTube topic labels as stored in the index — e.g. 'Knowledge' for educational content, 'Video game culture' for gaming, 'Lifestyle (sociology)' for lifestyle). Values are case-sensitive and must come from the enum; multiple values are OR-ed.

longAvgDuration7dMin.description: Minimum average long-form video duration over the trailing 7 days (seconds).

longAvgDuration7dMax.description: Maximum average long-form video duration over the trailing 7 days (seconds).

longAvgDuration30dMin.description: Minimum average long-form video duration over the trailing 30 days (seconds).

longAvgDuration30dMax.description: Maximum average long-form video duration over the trailing 30 days (seconds).

longAvgDuration1yMin.description: Minimum average long-form video duration over the trailing year (seconds).

longAvgDuration1yMax.description: Maximum average long-form video duration over the trailing year (seconds).

shortAvgDuration7dMin.description: Minimum average short-form video duration over the trailing 7 days (seconds).

shortAvgDuration7dMax.description: Maximum average short-form video duration over the trailing 7 days (seconds).

shortAvgDuration30dMin.description: Minimum average short-form video duration over the trailing 30 days (seconds).

shortAvgDuration30dMax.description: Maximum average short-form video duration over the trailing 30 days (seconds).

shortAvgDuration1yMin.description: Minimum average short-form video duration over the trailing year (seconds).

shortAvgDuration1yMax.description: Maximum average short-form video duration over the trailing year (seconds).

longVideoCount7dMin.description: Minimum number of long-form videos published in the trailing 7 days.

longVideoCount7dMax.description: Maximum number of long-form videos published in the trailing 7 days.

longVideoCount30dMin.description: Minimum number of long-form videos published in the trailing 30 days.

longVideoCount30dMax.description: Maximum number of long-form videos published in the trailing 30 days.

longVideoCount1yMin.description: Minimum number of long-form videos published in the trailing year.

longVideoCount1yMax.description: Maximum number of long-form videos published in the trailing year.

shortVideoCount7dMin.description: Minimum number of short-form videos published in the trailing 7 days.

shortVideoCount7dMax.description: Maximum number of short-form videos published in the trailing 7 days.

shortVideoCount30dMin.description: Minimum number of short-form videos published in the trailing 30 days.

shortVideoCount30dMax.description: Maximum number of short-form videos published in the trailing 30 days.

shortVideoCount1yMin.description: Minimum number of short-form videos published in the trailing year.

shortVideoCount1yMax.description: Maximum number of short-form videos published in the trailing year.

longViewCount7dMin.description: Minimum long-form view count accrued in the trailing 7 days.

longViewCount7dMax.description: Maximum long-form view count accrued in the trailing 7 days.

longViewCount30dMin.description: Minimum long-form view count accrued in the trailing 30 days.

longViewCount30dMax.description: Maximum long-form view count accrued in the trailing 30 days.

longViewCount1yMin.description: Minimum long-form view count accrued in the trailing year.

longViewCount1yMax.description: Maximum long-form view count accrued in the trailing year.

shortViewCount7dMin.description: Minimum short-form view count accrued in the trailing 7 days.

shortViewCount7dMax.description: Maximum short-form view count accrued in the trailing 7 days.

shortViewCount30dMin.description: Minimum short-form view count accrued in the trailing 30 days.

shortViewCount30dMax.description: Maximum short-form view count accrued in the trailing 30 days.

shortViewCount1yMin.description: Minimum short-form view count accrued in the trailing year.

shortViewCount1yMax.description: Maximum short-form view count accrued in the trailing year.

publishedAfter.description: Filter by channel creation date (ISO YYYY-MM-DD): created on or after this date.

publishedBefore.description: Filter by channel creation date (ISO YYYY-MM-DD): created on or before this date.

lastVideoPublishedAfter.description: Last uploaded video on or after this date (ISO YYYY-MM-DD) — active channels.

lastVideoPublishedBefore.description: Last uploaded video on or before this date (ISO YYYY-MM-DD).

nicheConfidenceMin.description: Minimum niche-classification confidence (0–1). Each channel is tagged with a niche and a confidence score; this floors out low-confidence / likely-misclassified channels so results stay on-topic. Defaults to 0.3 to favor cleanly-classified channels — set to 0 to disable the floor and include unclassified/low-confidence channels.

limit.description: Number of channels to return (max 50). For geo-local or language-specific discovery, request 20+ — relevant local channels may rank below globally popular ones.

sort.description: Result ordering: 'relevance' (default for text searches; ranks by semantic + lexical score), 'subscriberCount' (subscribers desc), 'subsGrowth30d' (30-day subscriber growth desc), 'viewsGrowth30d' (30-day view growth desc), or 'lastVideoPublished' (most recently active first). Pure-filter searches default to subscriberCount desc. Use 'relevance' for ALL topical queries including 'popular/famous/top channels in X' — relevance ranking already favors notable channels. Use field sorts only for explicit metric scans ('most subscribed regardless of topic fit', 'fastest growing'); pair growth sorts with subscriberCountMin (e.g. 1000) because growth percentages explode at tiny subscriber bases.

tools.name: vidiq_video_stats
tools.title: Video Stats History
tools.description:
Get historical statistics for a YouTube video over time, including views, likes, comments, and views per hour (VPH).

Use this tool to:
- Track how a video's performance changes over time
- Compare view velocity (VPH) across different periods
- Analyze engagement trends (likes, comments) for a specific video

Cost: 5 credit(s) per call.

granularity.description: Time granularity for stats data points

tools.name: vidiq_get_videos_by_ids
tools.title: Get Videos by IDs
tools.description:
Get detailed metadata for one or more YouTube videos by their IDs.

Use this tool to:
- Fetch video details such as title, description, tags, and category
- Get current statistics (views, likes, comments) for specific videos
- Look up publishing info, channel details, and thumbnails for videos
- Check topic categories for videos

Cost: 5 credit(s) per call.

videoIds.description: List of YouTube video IDs to fetch

tools.name: vidiq_youtube_search
tools.title: Search YouTube
tools.description:
Search all of YouTube for videos, channels, or playlists matching a query, with optional filters (topic, region, publish date, duration, channel). Use this when someone asks to "find videos about X", "search YouTube for Y", "find channels about Z", or wants results for a specific keyword.

Unlike vidiq_trending_videos (which returns what's hot right now) and vidiq_outliers (which finds videos over-performing relative to their channel), this is a general keyword search across the whole catalog. It returns raw YouTube search results, NOT keyword metrics — for search volume/competition use keyword research instead.

Use this tool to:
- Find videos, channels, or playlists by keyword (set 'type')
- Narrow results by topic, region, publish-date window, and (for videos) duration, definition (HD), or live/upcoming event type
- Search within a single channel by passing 'channelId'
- Sort by relevance, recency ('date'), or popularity ('viewCount')

Filters marked "Video only" require type='video'.

Cost: 5 credit(s) per call.

type.description: Kinds of result to return — any combination of video, channel, playlist. Defaults to all three. The video-only filters require type to be exactly ['video'].

order.description: Sort order. 'date' = newest, 'viewCount' = most viewed, 'videoCount' = channels with most videos.

publishedAfter.description: Only results published after this RFC 3339 timestamp (e.g. '2026-01-01T00:00:00Z')

publishedBefore.description: Only results published before this RFC 3339 timestamp

channelId.description: Restrict the search to a single channel's content

regionCode.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 region code to localize results (e.g. 'US', 'DE')

videoDuration.description: Length: short (<4min), medium (4-20min), long (>20min). Video only.

id.description: YouTube id of the video, channel, or playlist (depending on kind)

channelId.description: Channel id associated with this result, when available

tools.name: vidiq_get_channels_by_ids
tools.title: Get Channels by IDs
tools.description:
Get detailed information about YouTube channels by their IDs.

Use this tool to:
- Look up channel metadata (title, description, country) for one or more channels
- Get subscriber counts, view counts, and video counts
- Retrieve channel tags and topic categories
- Check channel thumbnails

Cost: 5 credit(s) per call.

channelIds.description: List of YouTube channel IDs to look up

tools.name: vidiq_balance
tools.title: Credits Balance
tools.description:
Check the user's current vidIQ credits balance.

vidIQ credits live in two buckets:
- A renewable bucket sized by the user's plan (Free/Boost/Max/etc) that refills each billing cycle.
- Zero or more add-on (bonus) credits. Add-on credits drain alongside renewable credits but do NOT refill.

Field semantics are in the output schema. Key invariant: totalCredits = renewableCredits + addOnCredits; maxRenewableCredits is the plan cap only.

Example — Boost user with an open promo grant:
  { type: 'limited', totalCredits: 1800,
    renewableCredits: 1500, maxRenewableCredits: 2000,
    renewableResetsAt: '2026-06-01T00:00:00Z',
    addOnCredits: 300, maxAddOnCredits: 500 }
Read as: 1,800 left = 1,500 of 2,000 renewable + 300 of a 500-credit bonus pool. Renews Jun 1.

Cost: 0 credit(s) per call.

type.description: 'limited' means the user has a finite renewable credit budget that refills each billing cycle. 'unlimited' means the user is on an unlimited plan (e.g. enterprise) and the renewable bucket does not decrement.

totalCredits.description: Total remaining credits the user can spend right now — renewableCredits plus addOnCredits. Null when type='unlimited'. This is the headline number to show in chat.

renewableCredits.description: Remaining credits in the renewable plan bucket alone — does NOT include add-on credits. Null when type='unlimited'.

maxRenewableCredits.description: The user's renewable plan cap (e.g. 150 for Free, 2000 for Boost, 6000 for Max). This is the amount that refills each billing cycle. Does NOT include add-on credits. Null when the plan has no cap.

renewableResetsAt.description: ISO timestamp when the renewable credit bucket next renews. Add-on credits do NOT renew; only the renewable bucket. Null when type='unlimited' or the backend did not return a renewal time.

addOnCredits.description: Remaining add-on (bonus) credits. Drain alongside renewable credits and do NOT refill on the billing cycle.

maxAddOnCredits.description: Initial total of all currently-active add-on credits — i.e. how much bonus credit the user originally received. Combine with addOnCredits to compute add-on usage.

tools.name: vidiq_user_channels
tools.title: User Channels
tools.description:
Get the list of YouTube channels authorized by the current user.

Cost: 0 credit(s) per call.

tools.name: vidiq_channel_videos
tools.title: Channel Videos
tools.description:
Get videos from a YouTube channel by format (long-form, Shorts, or live streams). Use this when you already know which channel to look at. To discover videos across many channels, use vidiq_outliers or vidiq_trending_videos.

Use this tool to:
- Browse a channel's recent uploads by video type (popular=false)
- Discover a channel's most popular videos by type (popular=true, default)
- Get video metadata including titles, publish dates, and thumbnails

Cost: 5 credit(s) per call.

videoFormat.description: Type of videos to retrieve: 'long' for long-form, 'short' for Shorts, 'live' for live streams

popular.description: When true (default), returns the most popular videos. When false, returns recent uploads.

tools.name: vidiq_video_transcript
tools.title: Video Transcript
tools.description:
Get the full transcript (captions) of a YouTube video.

Use this tool to:
- Analyze what a video talks about without watching it
- Extract key topics, quotes, or timestamps from a video
- Compare content across multiple videos by reading their transcripts
- Research video content for SEO or content strategy purposes

Cost: 5 credit(s) per call.

tools.name: vidiq_video_comments
tools.title: Video Comments
tools.description:
Get YouTube comment threads for a video or channel, including replies.

Provide exactly one of videoId or channelId.

Use this tool to:
- Understand audience sentiment and reactions to a video
- Find common questions or feedback themes in comments
- Browse recent comments across an entire channel
- Identify the most liked or discussed comments

Cost: 5 credit(s) per call.

videoId.description: YouTube video ID (e.g. 'dQw4w9WgXcQ'). Provide either videoId or channelId.

channelId.description: YouTube channel ID, handle (e.g. '@MrBeast'), or channel URL. Provide either videoId or channelId.

maxResult.description: Number of comment threads to return (1–100)

tools.name: vidiq_channel_performance_trends
tools.title: Channel Video Performance Trends
tools.description:
Get a channel's typical video performance curve — how views accumulate over time after publication.

Returns statistical aggregates (min, max, avg, median, percentiles) of view counts at various time intervals since publication, based on the channel's recent videos.

Use this tool to:
- Understand how quickly a channel's videos gain views after publishing
- Compare a channel's view velocity against benchmarks
- Identify whether a channel has strong initial traction or slow-burn growth

Cost: 5 credit(s) per call.

channelId.description: YouTube channel ID, handle (e.g. '@MrBeast'), or channel URL

tools.name: vidiq_channel_analytics
tools.title: Channel Analytics
tools.description:
Get YouTube Analytics data for a channel you own — views, watch time, subscribers gained, likes, comments, and more.

Supports flexible date ranges, metric selection, and dimensional breakdowns (by day, country, device type, traffic source, etc.).

Use this tool to:
- Analyze channel performance over a specific time period
- Break down metrics by day, country, device type, or traffic source
- Track subscriber growth, view counts, and engagement trends
- Compare performance across different dimensions

Note: Only available for channels authorized to your account.

Cost: 5 credit(s) per call.

startDate.description: Start date (YYYY-MM-DD). Defaults to 28 days ago

metrics.description: Metrics to retrieve. Defaults to views, estimatedMinutesWatched, subscribersGained, likes, comments

maxResults.description: Maximum number of rows to return

sort.description: Sort field, prefix with '-' for descending (e.g. '-views')

tools.name: vidiq_trend_categories
tools.title: Trend Categories
tools.description:
List all available trend category slugs with display names and descriptions. Use this as a reference before filtering outlier videos by trend category.

Cost: 0 credit(s) per call.

tools.name: vidiq_similar_channels
tools.title: Similar Channels
tools.description:
Find COMPETITORS of a channel — either the user's own channel or a specific named channel. Uses neural semantic search tuned for competitor matching; the user's own authorized channels are automatically excluded from results.

Use this tool ONLY when someone asks for competitors or similar channels relative to a channel:
- "Who are my competitors?" or "Find channels like mine" (the user's own channel — country/language are auto-detected from it)
- "Channels similar to [channel]", "Competitors of [channel]", or "Channels like @handle" (a named reference channel — see SIMILAR TO A SPECIFIC CHANNEL below)

Do NOT use this tool for generic channel discovery by topic, size, growth, or any other criteria ("find tech review channels", "top gaming channels in Brazil", "fastest growing fitness channels") — use vidiq_channel_search for that.

The 'niche' parameter is required — always provide a clear topic or content theme. Optionally narrow results with minSubscribers and maxSubscribers.

SIMILAR TO A SPECIFIC CHANNEL — when the user names a reference channel ("channels similar to Creator Insider", "competitors of MrBeast", "channels like @veritasium"), do NOT guess its characteristics. First resolve the channel with vidiq_channel_search (it returns channelId, niche, subNiches, subscriberCount, country, languages), then call this tool with:
- niche: copied verbatim from the reference channel's niche + subNiches, comma-separated (e.g. 'YouTube Growth, Monetization & SEO') — do NOT invent your own niche wording when the channel was found; only fall back to your own description if the channel was not found or its niche is empty
- country: the reference channel's country
- language: the first code from the reference channel's languages
- minSubscribers / maxSubscribers: a band around the reference channel's subscriberCount so results are in a similar league — roughly 10% of it as minSubscribers and 5x it as maxSubscribers (e.g. 300k subs → minSubscribers=30000, maxSubscribers=1500000), unless the user asks for a specific range
- excludeChannelIds: [the reference channel's channelId] — otherwise the channel appears in its own competitor list
Skipping this step produces noticeably worse, off-vibe results.

Results are ranked by neural similarity combining content overlap, audience size proximity, and niche alignment. Each result includes the channel's title, subscriber count, niches, languages, and country.

Optionally filter by country (ISO 3166-1 two-letter code) and/or language (ISO 639-1 code). When not provided, these are auto-detected from the user's authorized channel.

Cost: 5 credit(s) per call.

niche.description: The niche, topic, or content theme to search for similar channels in. Use natural language — e.g. 'tech reviews', 'vegan cooking tutorials', 'Minecraft let's plays', 'personal finance for millennials'. This is the primary search query and must always be provided.

minSubscribers.description: Lower bound for subscriber count. Use to filter out smaller channels — e.g. 10000 to only see channels with 10k+ subscribers. Use together with maxSubscribers to define a specific range.

maxSubscribers.description: Upper bound for subscriber count. Use to filter out larger channels — e.g. 1000000 to cap results at channels under 1M subscribers. Use together with minSubscribers to define a specific range.

country.description: Two-letter ISO 3166-1 country code to filter results by channel country — e.g. 'US', 'GB', 'BR', 'JP'. When omitted, the country is auto-detected from the user's authorized channel (if any).

language.description: Two-letter ISO 639-1 language code to filter results by channel language — e.g. 'en', 'es', 'pt', 'ja'. Only a single language is accepted. When omitted, the language is auto-detected from the user's authorized channel (if any).

size.description: Number of similar channels to return. Defaults to 30, maximum 50.

excludeChannelIds.description: Channel IDs to exclude from results. When searching for competitors of a specific named channel, ALWAYS pass that channel's channelId here so it does not appear in its own competitor list. The user's own authorized channels are excluded automatically and do not need to be listed.

tools.name: vidiq_submit_feedback
tools.title: Submit Feedback
tools.description:
Submit feedback about the vidIQ MCP — feature requests, bug reports, improvement suggestions, or general comments. Users can proactively share feedback, and you (the AI) should also call this tool to relay user input when the conversation reveals an unmet need, a broken experience, or a pain point — even if the user doesn't explicitly ask to submit feedback.

Cost: 0 credit(s) per call.

type.description: The kind of feedback: feature_request for new tools/capabilities, bug_report for something broken, improvement for enhancing an existing tool, general for anything else

tool_name.description: The relevant existing tool name, if this feedback is about a specific tool (e.g. 'vidiq_keyword_research')

description.description: What you're requesting, reporting, or suggesting — and why

use_case.description: A specific workflow or scenario for additional context

tools.name: vidiq_score_title
tools.title: Score Title
tools.description:
Score a YouTube video title for click-through-rate potential. Returns a score (0-100) indicating how compelling and clickable the title is. Use this to compare title variations and pick the best-performing option.

Cost: 5 credit(s) per call.

tools.name: vidiq_score_thumbnail
tools.title: Score Thumbnail
tools.description:
Score a YouTube video thumbnail for click-through-rate potential. Returns a score (0-100) with detailed feedback on strengths and improvements. Provide a video ID and title; optionally supply a custom thumbnail image URL to score instead of the current YouTube thumbnail.

Cost: 5 credit(s) per call.

title.description: Video title (used together with the thumbnail for scoring)

image.description: Thumbnail image URL. If omitted, the current YouTube thumbnail is used.

tools.name: vidiq_generate_titles
tools.title: Generate Titles
tools.description:
Generate scored YouTube title suggestions.

Provide at least one of `videoId`, `title`, or `description`. With
only `videoId`, the current title/description are fetched from YouTube.

Cost: 5 credit(s) per call.

videoId.description: YouTube video ID (e.g. 'dQw4w9WgXcQ'). When provided, the tool fetches the current title/description from yt-proxy to ground generation. Optional when title and/or description are supplied directly.

title.description: Current/working video title. When omitted but videoId is provided, fetched from yt-proxy. Caller-supplied value takes precedence.

description.description: Current video description. When omitted but videoId is provided, fetched from yt-proxy. Caller-supplied value takes precedence.

numTitles.description: How many title candidates to generate (default: 3)

type.description: Video format hint. Affects the generation prompt and scoring rubric. When omitted, upstream infers from duration and otherwise treats the video as long-form.

language.description: Target language for generated titles and the analysis pass (e.g. 'en', 'pt-BR'). Defaults to English when omitted.

regionCode.description: 2-letter ISO country code (e.g. 'US', 'BR'). Normalized to uppercase. Used by upstream when fetching competitive context.

scoreThreshold.description: Return only suggestions with score >= threshold. Thresholds above 70 on long-form videos may legitimately yield no results, and the filter applies before the numTitles top-K slice (so the response may contain fewer items than numTitles).

previousTitles.description: Recent titles from the same channel. Used by upstream to reduce repetition and improve novelty. Capped at 20 entries x 200 chars; duplicates removed.

competitorTitles.description: Seed titles for the competitive context block. When provided, upstream skips its Outliers lookup and uses these directly — useful for deterministic output or when the caller already has a curated competitor set. Capped at 20 entries x 200 chars; duplicates removed.

analysisSummary.description: Pre-computed analysis summary. When provided, upstream skips its own analysis pass (transcript/frames → Gemini) and uses this directly. Useful when chaining after a tool that already produced video understanding. Capped at 4000 chars.

tools.name: vidiq_generate_thumbnail
tools.title: Generate Thumbnail
tools.description:
Generate a YouTube thumbnail

Provide at least one of videoId, title, description, or userQuery (custom input).
With only videoId, the current title/description and the default YouTube thumbnail are pulled automatically.
Returns the generated thumbnail (embedded inline as an image) plus its URL, a self-score, and feedback.

Cost: 22 credit(s) per call.

videoId.description: YouTube video ID (e.g. 'dQw4w9WgXcQ'). When provided, title, description, and currentThumbnail are auto-filled from YouTube unless the caller supplies them explicitly. Optional when at least one of title, description, or userQuery is given.

title.description: Video title used for grounding. When omitted but videoId is provided, fetched from yt-proxy. Caller-supplied value wins.

description.description: Video description used for grounding. When omitted but videoId is provided, fetched from yt-proxy. Caller-supplied value wins.

transcript.description: Optional transcript text. Used for subject detection in Quick Generate and as context in Pro Generate. Pair with vidiq_video_transcript when available.

userQuery.description: Natural-language creative direction (e.g. 'show the host pointing at a glowing dollar sign'). Highest-priority generation signal upstream.

currentThumbnail.description: URL of an existing/current thumbnail to reference for composition. When omitted but videoId is provided, auto-filled with the video's maxresdefault YouTube thumbnail.

subjectImage.description: URL of a subject/person image. Highest-priority likeness reference for the main face in the thumbnail.

referenceImages.description: URLs of reference thumbnails to emulate style/composition. Presence of this field switches the request out of Quick Generate into the Pro / Improve flow upstream. Capped at 10 entries; duplicates removed.

score.description: Overall quality score (0–100) returned by the previous scoring call.

pointImpact.description: Point delta this finding contributed to the overall score (when available).

strengths.description: Findings to preserve in the next iteration.

improvements.description: Findings to fix in the next iteration.

feedback.description: Optional scorer feedback from a prior thumbnail (typically the output of vidiq_score_thumbnail). When provided alongside referenceImages, triggers the feedback-iteration flow.

tools.name: vidiq_refine_thumbnail
tools.title: Refine Thumbnail
tools.description:
Change or improve a YouTube thumbnail by describing what you want in plain words.

Give it the thumbnail to change (sourceThumbnail) and say what to change (instructions) — for example "make the text bigger" or "change the background to a football stadium".
You get back the updated thumbnail (shown right here) plus a link to it.

Cost: 22 credit(s) per call.

sourceThumbnail.description: Link to the thumbnail you want to change. Usually a thumbnail you just made with vidiq_generate_thumbnail, but any thumbnail image link works.

instructions.description: What you want changed, in plain words — for example 'make the background darker' or 'make the face bigger'. Describe the change, not the whole picture.

originalConcept.description: Optional. A short description of what the original thumbnail shows. Helps keep the result on-topic. If left out, your instructions are used.

videoId.description: Optional. The YouTube video this thumbnail is for.

subjectImage.description: Optional. Link to a photo of the person who should be the main face in the thumbnail.

referenceImages.description: Optional. Links to other thumbnails whose style you'd like to copy (up to 10).

mask.description: Optional. To change only one area and leave the rest untouched, provide a base64 image that marks the area to change.

tools.name: vidiq_video_watch
tools.title: Watch Video
tools.description:
Watch a YouTube video end-to-end and return a structured markdown walkthrough.

Use this tool to:
- Understand what happens visually in a video (not just what is said) — useful when transcripts miss context
- Generate scene-by-scene breakdowns with timestamps for editing or analysis
- Answer specific questions about a video by passing a custom `prompt`
- Compare visual structure across videos for SEO / hook research

The response is **markdown** in the `analysisText` field — scene headers with time ranges followed by a summary.

Note: prefer `vidiq_video_transcript` when you only need spoken words, since it is faster and cheaper.

Cost: 25 credit(s) per call.

video.description: YouTube video ID (e.g. 'dQw4w9WgXcQ') or canonical watch/shorts/youtu.be URL. URLs are normalised to the bare ID before processing.

prompt.description: Optional custom analysis instruction. If omitted, a scene-by-scene markdown walkthrough is produced (### Scene N: Label headers with mm:ss timestamps + ### Summary bullets). Markdown structure is enforced regardless — do not ask for JSON.

tools.name: vidiq_ig_profile
tools.title: Instagram Profile
tools.description:
Fetch public Instagram profile by handle: bio, follower/following counts, post count, verification, external URL, with the profile picture returned inline.

DO NOT USE FOR:
- Profile search/discovery — you must already have a handle.
- Reels — use `vidiq_ig_profile_reels` (up to 6, popularity-sorted, no pagination).
- Stories, Highlights, IGTV, private-account data.
- Engagement-rate or growth analytics — snapshot only.

Cost: 5 credit(s) per call.

handle.description: Instagram handle (with or without leading @). 1-30 chars; letters/digits/dot/underscore.

tools.name: vidiq_ig_profile_reels
tools.title: Instagram Profile Reels
tools.description:
Fetch a creator's reels by handle: shortcode, caption, play/like/comment counts, duration, timestamp, with each reel's cover returned inline as an image. Returns up to 6 reels drawn from recent activity, sorted by play count (popularity-first within the returned set, not strictly newest). Feeds shortcodes into `vidiq_ig_reel_watch`.

DO NOT USE FOR:
- More than 6 reels, or pagination — not supported.
- Strict newest-first ordering — sort is popularity within recent activity.
- Image posts, carousels, Stories.
- Account, creator, influencer, or competitor-account discovery - use `vidiq_ig_accounts_from_outliers`.
- Individual cross-creator reel examples or topic search - use `vidiq_ig_outlier_reels_search` (cannot filter by handle).
- Critiquing reel content — use `vidiq_ig_reel_watch`.

Cost: 5 credit(s) per call.

handle.description: Instagram handle (with or without leading @). 1-30 chars; letters/digits/dot/underscore.

tools.name: vidiq_ig_outlier_reels_search
tools.title: Instagram Outlier Reels
tools.description:
Find high-performing Instagram reels (outliers beating the creator's median by a significant margin). Returns reel IDs, URLs, counts, reel-median score. Extract shortcode from `url` for `vidiq_ig_reel_watch`.

Use `vidiq_ig_accounts_from_outliers` instead when the user asks for Instagram accounts, creators, influencers, competitors, or partners to discover. This tool is for individual reel examples after account candidates or patterns are known.

How to call:
- `audienceQuery` is required and is a separate rescore layer for demographic fit. Format:
  `Culture/Region: …; Global: <true|false>; Demographics: …;`
  Example: `Culture/Region: UK/British; Global: false; Demographics: working professional men 25-45, fat loss, busy lifestyle;`
  Derive from bio + recent reels (`vidiq_ig_profile` / `vidiq_ig_profile_reels` first), not from handle. If audience is vague, confirm with the user before searching.
- Pick `embeddingType`:
  - `description` — caption + hashtags; topical asks. `query` reads like a caption.
  - `concept` — one-line summary; niche / competitor search. `query` describes the reel.
  - `hook` — `Hook visual: …; Hook text: …; Hook audio: …;`; replicating an opening.
  - `format` — subject-free production template, ideally <=6 words. Prefer second-pass use: first find relevant `concept`/`hook` hits, read `reelSummary.format.template`, then rerun `format` with exact template phrases. Do not use `format` alone for topic/audience fit.
- Parallelize across embedding types with the same `audienceQuery`. Each parallel call must use a different `embeddingType` or a clearly different topic intent — no duplicates.
- Defaults to reels posted in the last 30 days. Sparse niche → widen `datePostedAfter`. Small-creator replication → cap `followersMax` near 10× the target. Non-English → set `descriptionLanguage` (ISO 639-1).

Cost: 5 credit(s) per call.

tools.name: vidiq_ig_accounts_from_outliers
tools.title: Instagram Accounts From Outliers
tools.description:
Find Instagram creator/account candidates from existing outlier reel discovery.

Use this when the user asks for Instagram accounts, creators, competitors, influencers, partners, or examples to study. The api-ts service groups outlier reels into accounts and verifies every candidate against profile/recent content before returning it.

How to call:
- `niche` uses the same concept-search shape as Instagram outlier search: write a one-line description of the reel/content niche that should represent matching accounts. Examples: `holistic psychology reels about nervous system regulation`; `fitness reels with practical fat-loss routines and progress breakdowns`.
  Put audience demographics/culture in `audienceQuery`.
- `audienceQuery` is required and must use the same shape as Instagram outlier search:
  `Culture/Region: ...; Global: <true|false>; Demographics: ...;`
  Example: `Culture/Region: UK/British; Global: false; Demographics: working professional men 25-45, fat loss, busy lifestyle;`
  Derive from the user's stated target audience or from bio + recent reels (`vidiq_ig_profile` / `vidiq_ig_profile_reels` first), not from handle. If audience is vague, confirm with the user before searching.
- Use optional `followersMin` / `followersMax` when account size matters.

Cost: 10 credit(s) per call.

niche.description: One-line description of the Instagram reel/content niche to search for.

tools.name: vidiq_ig_reel_watch
tools.title: Watch Instagram Reel
tools.description:
Watch an Instagram reel and return its cover image inline plus a markdown critique. Default rates Stop / Gap / Momentum / Feeling / Reach (Strong/Moderate/Weak) with bulleted reasoning, transcript (if speech), and numbered Top Improvements.

Prefer the default `prompt` — it's the tuned production critique and matches almost every reel-analysis intent. Override only for genuinely different jobs (transcript-only, single-aspect questions).

DO NOT USE FOR:
- Image posts or carousels — video reels only.
- Multiple reels per call — call in parallel to compare.
- Private or audience-restricted reels — return a typed error; don't retry.

Cost: 10 credit(s) per call.

reel.description: Instagram reel shortcode (e.g. 'C9zX1aBcD2E') or full URL (https://www.instagram.com/reel/<shortcode>/ or /p/<shortcode>/). URLs are normalised to the bare shortcode.

prompt.description: Optional custom instruction. **Prefer omitting** — the default Five Forces critique is the tuned production prompt and is right for almost every reel-analysis task. Override only for genuinely different jobs (transcript-only, single-aspect questions, non-critique walkthroughs). Markdown output regardless — do not ask for JSON.

tools.name: vidiq_list_competitors
tools.title: List Competitors
tools.description:
List the YouTube channels the user is tracking as competitors of one of their own channels.

Returns only channel IDs. To get titles, thumbnails, subscriber counts, etc., pass the IDs to vidiq_get_channels_by_ids.

The youtubeChannelId must be a channel the user has authorized — use vidiq_user_channels to discover them.

Cost: 0 credit(s) per call.

youtubeChannelId.description: YouTube channel ID (e.g. 'UCxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx') of one of the user's own authorized channels. Use vidiq_user_channels first if you don't know which channels the user owns.

tools.name: vidiq_update_competitors
tools.title: Update Competitors
tools.description:
Follow or unfollow competitor channels for one of the user's own YouTube channels.

- youtubeChannelId must be a channel the user has authorized (call vidiq_user_channels to discover).
- follow / unfollow are arrays of YouTube channel IDs — they must not overlap; at least one must be non-empty.
- Plan-based caps apply. Adding above the cap fails. Returns the resulting tracked-competitors list (channel IDs only).

Example arguments: { "youtubeChannelId": "UCowner123", "follow": ["UCcompA"], "unfollow": [] }

Cost: 0 credit(s) per call.

youtubeChannelId.description: YouTube channel ID (starts with 'UC', e.g. 'UCabc123') of one of the user's own authorized channels. Call vidiq_user_channels first if unknown.

follow.description: Array of YouTube channel IDs to start tracking as competitors, e.g. ["UCabc123","UCdef456"].

unfollow.description: Array of YouTube channel IDs to stop tracking, e.g. ["UCabc123"].

tools.name: vidiq_generate_broll
tools.title: Find Stock B-roll
tools.description:
Find free stock B-roll video clips.

Provide a search query (optionally orientation and min/max duration filters).
Returns up to 4 clips with a direct mp4 link, a preview image, dimensions, and the
photographer credit. Attribution is required: always credit the photographer and
link back to the source page when using a clip.

Cost: 1 credit(s) per call.

query.description: Search term for the stock B-roll footage, e.g. 'city skyline at night'.

orientation.description: Optional orientation filter. Use 'portrait' for Shorts/Reels, 'landscape' otherwise.

minDurationSeconds.description: Optional minimum clip duration in seconds.

maxDurationSeconds.description: Optional maximum clip duration in seconds.

url.description: Direct mp4 link to the clip.

pageUrl.description: Source page URL — link back to it for attribution.

photographer.description: Creator name — credit them when using the clip.

tools.name: vidiq_voiceover_list_voices
tools.title: List Voiceover Voices
tools.description:
List the available voiceover voices.

Returns each voice's id, name, category, description, and an optional audio preview URL.
The user's own cloned/designed voices have isCustom=true and are listed first; prefer
them when the user asks for "my voice". Use the returned voiceId with vidiq_voiceover_generate.

Cost: 0 credit(s) per call.

previewUrl.description: URL to an audio preview of the voice, if available.

isCustom.description: True if this is the user's own cloned/designed voice (listed first). Prefer these when the user refers to 'my voice'.

tools.name: vidiq_voiceover_generate
tools.title: Generate Voiceover
tools.description:
Generate a voiceover MP3 from a script.

Provide the script text (max 5000 characters) and a voiceId
from vidiq_voiceover_list_voices. Returns the audio duration, character count,
and a temporary download URL for the MP3. Pass output='url_and_audio' to
additionally receive the MP3 inline as a base64 audio block.

Cost: 14 credit(s) per 1000 characters of script (rounded up).

script.description: The text to synthesize into speech.

output.description: How to return the audio. The MP3 is always uploaded and a temporary download URL returned. 'url_only' (default) returns just that URL — use this for hosts that can't render inline audio. 'url_and_audio' additionally attaches the MP3 inline as a base64 audio block.

voiceoverId.description: Stable id for the generated voiceover.

durationSeconds.description: Length of the generated audio in seconds.

audioUrl.description: Temporary download URL for the MP3.

tools.name: vidiq_compose
tools.title: Compose Video
tools.description:
Compose a short video from scenes (video clips and/or images), an optional
voiceover, background music, and text overlays, then render it to an MP4.

Provide ordered `scenes` (each with an HTTPS `source` and `duration`) and an
output `format`. The video's length is the sum of the scene durations; audio
longer than that is cut off at the end, so size scenes to fit any voiceover.
Limits: at most 240s of total video, 50 scenes, and 50 overlays.

Fixed behavior in this version: video clips play from their beginning with
their native audio muted (use `voiceover`/`music` for sound), and every scene
is scaled and center-cropped to fill the output frame, so sources whose aspect
ratio differs from `format` lose their edges.

Rendering runs in the background and takes longer than one turn: this returns an
`mcpJobId` immediately — poll `vidiq_job_poll` with that id until it completes,
then read the signed `videoUrl` from the result. Credits are charged once on
submit and automatically refunded if the render fails.

Cost: 1 credit(s) per 4s of output video (rounded up), minimum 2 credits.

source.description: HTTPS URL of the clip (video) or image for this scene.

duration.description: On-screen duration of this scene, in seconds.

type.description: Whether `source` is a video clip or a still image.

transitionIn.description: Transition played as this scene enters (e.g. fade).

x.description: Normalized focal point X (0–1) the zoom centers on. 0.5 = horizontal center.

y.description: Normalized focal point Y (0–1) the zoom centers on. 0.5 = vertical center.

to.description: Ending keyframe. For a plain center zoom-in use from {scale 1, x 0.5, y 0.5} to {scale 1.2, x 0.5, y 0.5}.

kenBurns.description: Ken Burns pan/zoom for image scenes (ignored for video). x/y are the focal point the zoom centers on (0.5/0.5 = center), not a pan offset.

scenes.description: Ordered scenes that make up the video. At least one is required; at most 50. Total duration across all scenes must not exceed 240 seconds.

format.description: Output aspect: vertical 9:16, landscape 16:9, or square 1:1.

audioUrl.description: HTTPS URL of the voiceover MP3 (from vidiq_voiceover_generate).

voiceover.description: Voiceover audio, played from the start. The video's length is the sum of scene durations — audio longer than that is cut off at the end, so size scenes to fit it.

start.description: When the overlay appears, in seconds from the start.

duration.description: How long the overlay stays on screen, in seconds.

overlays.description: Text overlays burned onto the video. At most 50.

captions.description: Caption style preset. (v1 selects a style only; caption text is not yet wired.)

mcpJobId.description: Opaque job id. Pass this to `vidiq_job_poll` to check progress and get the result.

status.description: Always 'inprogress' on submit — the work runs asynchronously.

message.description: Human-readable next-step instruction for the caller.

tools.name: vidiq_voiceover_clone
tools.title: Clone Voiceover Voice
tools.description:
Clone a voice from an audio sample and save it to the user's voice library.

Provide a base64-encoded audio sample (a 60-600s MP3/WAV/M4A clip of a single
speaker who has consented to being cloned) and a display name. Returns the new
voiceId, which can be used with vidiq_voiceover_generate. Each user may keep at
most 3 custom voices. Video samples are not accepted — extract the audio first.

Cost: 50 credit(s) per call.

name.description: Display name for the cloned voice (e.g. 'My Voice').

audioBase64.description: Base64-encoded audio sample of a single consenting speaker. Must be a 60-600s MP3, WAV, or M4A clip. Video is not accepted — extract the audio first.

contentType.description: MIME type of the sample (e.g. 'audio/mpeg', 'audio/wav'). Defaults to audio/mpeg.

voiceId.description: Identifier of the cloned voice; pass to vidiq_voiceover_generate.

previewUrl.description: URL to an audio preview of the cloned voice, if available.

requiresVerification.description: True if the voice was saved but flagged for follow-up verification.

tools.name: vidiq_voiceover_clone_start
tools.title: Clone Voice from YouTube
tools.description:
Start cloning the speaker's voice from a YouTube video.

Provide a public YouTube URL and a name for the voice. This runs asynchronously:
it returns an `mcpJobId` immediately — poll `vidiq_job_poll` with that id
until it completes, then use the returned `voiceId`.

Credits are charged on submit and automatically refunded if the clone fails.

Cost: 1 credit(s) per call.

youtubeUrl.description: Public YouTube URL of a video whose voice should be cloned (watch / youtu.be / shorts URL).

name.description: A name for the cloned voice, used to label it once the clone completes.

mcpJobId.description: Opaque job id. Pass this to `vidiq_job_poll` to check progress and get the result.

status.description: Always 'inprogress' on submit — the work runs asynchronously.

message.description: Human-readable next-step instruction for the caller.

tools.name: vidiq_jobs_list
tools.title: List Jobs
tools.description:
List your asynchronous vidIQ jobs, newest first.

Filter by `toolName` and/or `status`. To find jobs that are still running,
filter `status: 'inprogress'`. Use the returned `mcpJobId` with
`vidiq_job_poll` to check a job's result.

This tool is free.

Cost: 0 credit(s) per call.

toolName.description: Filter to jobs created by this tool (e.g. 'vidiq_voiceover_clone_start').

status.description: Filter by status. Omit for all. You usually want 'inprogress' to find jobs still running.

limit.description: Max jobs to return (1-50, default 20). Newest first.

createdAt.description: ISO timestamp the job was submitted.

tools.name: vidiq_job_poll
tools.title: Check Job Status
tools.description:
Check the status of an asynchronous vidIQ job and retrieve its result.

Pass the `mcpJobId` returned by a `*_start` tool. Poll periodically until
`status` is no longer 'inprogress':
- 'completed' → the work is done; read `result`.
- 'failed'/'expired' → it did not finish; the credits were refunded (`refunded: true`).

This tool is free.

Cost: 0 credit(s) per call.

mcpJobId.description: The job id returned by a `*_start` tool (e.g. `vidiq_voiceover_clone_start`).

toolName.description: The tool that created this job.

status.description: inprogress = still running; completed = done, see `result`; failed = downstream failed; expired = no result within the time limit; refunded = a failed/expired charge was returned.

result.description: The tool-specific result when status='completed'; null otherwise.

refunded.description: True when the credits for this job were refunded (failed/expired jobs).

message.description: Human-readable status note (e.g. why it failed). Null when not applicable.

tools.name: vidiq_generate_clips
tools.title: Generate Video Clips
tools.description:
Turn a long video into short, vertical clips.

Provide either a YouTube link (videoUrl) or an already-hosted file (uploadedVideoUrl + videoDuration + videoFilename). Optionally steer selection with a prompt, set clipDuration, restrict the source to a section with processingStartSeconds/processingEndSeconds (both required together, span >= 30s), and pick a captionStyle preset (or "none" to turn captions off). Captions use the "Loud & Clear" style by default.
This runs asynchronously: it returns an `mcpJobId` immediately — poll `vidiq_job_poll` with that id until it is "completed" to get the clips. If generation fails, that poll refunds the credits.

Credits are charged per minute of source video on submit and automatically refunded if generation fails.

Cost: 9 credit(s) per minute of source video (rounded up).

videoUrl.description: YouTube video URL to clip. Provide this OR uploadedVideoUrl, not both. Duration is fetched automatically.

uploadedVideoUrl.description: URL of an already-hosted video file (http/https/gs) to clip. Requires videoDuration and videoFilename.

videoDuration.description: Source video length in seconds. Required when using uploadedVideoUrl.

videoFilename.description: Original filename of the uploaded video. Required when using uploadedVideoUrl.

prompt.description: Optional. What to focus on when picking moments, e.g. 'funniest parts'.

clipDuration.description: Optional. Target length of each clip in seconds (0–120).

videoLanguage.description: Optional. Language code of the spoken audio, used for captions/transcription.

imgUrl.description: Optional. Image URL to use as an overlay/branding asset on the clips.

captionStyle.description: Caption style for the burned-in subtitles. Pick a named preset or "none" to disable captions. Defaults to "Loud & Clear" when omitted.

processingStartSeconds.description: Optional. Only consider the source video from this timestamp (seconds). Must be paired with processingEndSeconds.

processingEndSeconds.description: Optional. Only consider the source video up to this timestamp (seconds). Must be greater than processingStartSeconds and span at least 30s.

mcpJobId.description: Opaque job id. Pass this to `vidiq_job_poll` to check progress and get the result.

status.description: Always 'inprogress' on submit — the work runs asynchronously.

message.description: Human-readable next-step instruction for the caller.

tools.name: vidiq_generate_video
tools.title: Generate Video
tools.description:
Generate a short video from a text prompt.

Pick a model (default sora-2), resolution and duration valid for that model, then describe the video in prompt. Optionally steer with an aspectRatio, seed image-to-video with startFrameB64/endFrameB64, or remix an existing result with remixVideoId/remixProjectId.
This runs asynchronously: it returns an `mcpJobId` immediately — poll `vidiq_job_poll` with that id until it is "completed" to get the video URL. If generation fails, that poll refunds the credits.

Credits are charged on submit and automatically refunded if generation fails.

Cost: scales with duration (seconds) × the model's per-second rate × 20 credits, quoted exactly at submit.

prompt.description: What the video should depict (1–4000 chars).

model.description: Generation model. Each model allows specific durations/resolutions.

resolution.description: Output resolution. Must be valid for the chosen model (e.g. sora-2 → 720p).

duration.description: Clip length in seconds. Must be a valid duration for the model (e.g. sora-2 → 4/8/12).

aspectRatio.description: Optional. Frame aspect ratio; defaults per-model when omitted.

remixVideoId.description: Optional. Remix an existing generated video by id. Mutually exclusive with remixProjectId and image frames.

remixProjectId.description: Optional. Remix an existing project by id. Mutually exclusive with remixVideoId and image frames.

startFrameB64.description: Optional. Base64 first frame for image-to-video (JPEG/PNG/WebP, ≤10MB). A data: prefix is stripped server-side.

endFrameB64.description: Optional. Base64 last frame (JPEG/PNG/WebP, ≤10MB). Not supported for Sora models.

mcpJobId.description: Opaque job id. Pass this to `vidiq_job_poll` to check progress and get the result.

status.description: Always 'inprogress' on submit — the work runs asynchronously.

message.description: Human-readable next-step instruction for the caller.
